statement: Organic farming in Iceland is minimal, covering less than 1% of agricultural land as of 2024, compared to an EU average of approximately 10% (2022) with an EU target of 25% by 2030 under the Farm to Fork Strategy. Iceland has no specific national organic farming strategy. The EU's Green Deal and Farm to Fork Strategy set binding targets for pesticide reduction (50% by 2030), organic land share, and nutrient loss reduction that would apply to Iceland upon EU membership.
source_name: Eurostat — Organic farming statistics; Icelandic organic certification body (Vottunarstofan Tún); European Commission Farm to Fork Strategy
caveats: Iceland's low organic share partly reflects the extensive nature of farming (sheep grazing on rangeland uses few inputs already). Much Icelandic agriculture is de facto low-input but not formally certified organic. The relevance of EU pesticide targets is limited since Iceland already uses very low amounts of pesticides due to climate. Farm to Fork targets are under revision and may change before any Icelandic accession.

related_entries.statement: Approximately 20% of Iceland's total land area (about 20,000 km²) is classified as agricultural land, of which the vast majority is rangeland used for sheep grazing rather than arable land. Only about 1.2% of Iceland (approximately 1,300 km²) is cultivated hay fields. Centuries of sheep grazing have contributed to significant soil erosion and vegetation loss — an estimated 40% of Iceland's original vegetation cover has been lost since settlement in the 9th century. The Soil Conservation Service of Iceland (Landgræðslan) leads extensive revegetation programmes.

Iceland's agricultural sector contributed approximately 1.0–1.2% of GDP in 2024 and employed around 3,500 people (roughly 1.7% of the labour force). The sector is dominated by sheep farming (approximately 450,000 sheep), dairy production (approximately 30,000 cattle), and greenhouse horticulture (using geothermal energy). Iceland has approximately 2,300 farms, with average farm size significantly smaller than the EU average.

citations.report_title: 390.000 hektarar af landbúnaðarlandi breytast í skóg og votlendi

claims.claim_text: Í gegnum sameiginlega landbúnaðarstefnu ESB er veittur fjárhagslegur stuðningur til að hjálpa bændum að breyta landnotkun, fjárfesta í nýrri tækni og draga úr umhverfisáhrifum framleiðslunnar.

citations.report_title: Ræða þarf alla kaflana á nýjan leik

claims.claim_text: Mikill munur var á stjórnsýslu landbúnaðarmála á Íslandi og kröfum ESB, bæði hvað varðar stefnu og framkvæmd.
